why: A and B both ground to the character-handling caveat (K69038629) that is central to the reference, while C omits it and cites only K2167; B edges A with a clearer worked example.

A is faithful to the sparse reference and cites the correct DevCentral PDF source, whereas B invents a 'cache module check' step not in the reference and C mislabels VPE as 'Virtual Proxy Enforcement'.
B is fully grounded and concise; A is most complete but slightly long, while C fabricates EP-EP/TP-EP topology names not present in the reference.
A correctly surfaces the kubevirt device/hugepages 'Insufficient device' details grounded in the reference, making it the most complete and accurate.
All three correctly identify Bug ID 1114089 and are essentially identical; A is marginally the cleanest phrasing.
B gives correct GUI plus tmsh/iControl REST with valid syntax and RFC5737 examples, while C invents a 'Remote HTTP/S' option and a malformed tmsh receive-string syntax.
A and B describe the APM-before-load-balancing flow accurately; C mislabels VPE as 'Virtual Proxy Engine' and A is the tightest of the accurate two.
All three are accurate from real SSLO knowledge; B best explains service chaining and dynamic steering without padding.
All correctly cite CPU/memory/storage exhaustion; A is the most direct and well-structured without inventing specifics.
All three honestly decline to fabricate a bug ID (correct behavior in cb mode); A is the cleanest, most concise refusal.
